articles.title: Trust
articles.author: Hazel S. Washburn
articles.complete_text:
TRUST
By Hazel S. Washburn of Kirtland, New Mexico

(These stanzas Received Honorable Mention in the Eliza Roxey Snow Prize Memorial Contest)

Dear Heart, the day is long,
Now you're away;
The Sun shines on, but still
My world is gray.
I miss your dear, kind face
And loving way.

So swift the summons came that called you on,
I scarcely realized that you were gone,
And I must bear the heavy load alone.

And yet, Dear Heart, I know
That God is just.
I know he called you home,
Because he must.
And I can only wait, and pray,
And trust.
articles.notes: This is a complete poem by Hazel S. Washburn that received honorable mention in the Eliza Roxey Snow Prize Memorial Contest. It's a standalone literary work that should be extracted.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of legitimate miscellaneous material including: table of contents for June 1924 issue, publication information, various advertisements (Carnation Milk, temple clothes, flowers, garments, etc.), and the beginning of conference minutes that appear to be cut off. This is appropriate Misc content that should not be extracted as standalone articles.
